examples: They fossilise an industrial structure that needs to change.
Not only words and epithets fossilise, whole situations can fossilise too.
The brain itself does not fossilise, but the inside of the cranium retains an impression of its contours.
Since the vocal tract consists of soft tissue (and therefore does not fossilise), reconstructing it depends on extrapolating from measurements made at the bases of fossil skulls.
And the conservatism of those who want to fossilise it.
